entry.title: What’s New in My Home SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 Release?
entry.content:
My Home already serves as a centralized entry point for business users by providing immediate access to key insights, supporting the efficient management of tasks and situations, and enabling users to organize their favorite pages and apps. This helps streamline daily operations and reduces the time spent navigating across the system. The 2602 release builds on this foundation by enhancing how information is delivered, personalized, and consumed—ensuring that users can focus on what matters most.  

The 2602 release of My Home in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition introduces a range of new delivered and beta features, along with meaningful UX enhancements designed to elevate the overall user experience. In this blog, we’ll explore what’s new in this release and how these updates help business users work more efficiently and stay better informed. 

 

Mandatory News – Ensuring critical information is seen by all business users  

With the 2508 release, administrators gained the ability to deliver timely and targeted updates directly to business users through the My Home centralized news feed. The 2602 release takes this a step further by introducing mandatory news articles. News articles containing essential company information can now be marked as required reading. Business users must acknowledge these articles, confirming that they have read and understood the content. This feature plays a critical role in ensuring compliance with legal, safety, and policy-related requirements. It also helps eliminate knowledge gaps by ensuring that all employees receive the same information. During periods of change—such as the rollout of new policies or process updates—mandatory reading ensures that users understand both the reasons for the change and its potential impact, improving alignment across the organization. 

 An example of a news article that is required reading. Alt Text: A screenshot of My Home with a pop-over “Required Reading” that is also highlighted with “Contains Critical News”. The news article has an image at the top, followed by text showing the first part of the article. The “Done” button is greyed out, the “Remind me later” button is visible.

 

The Manage News app has a toggle to mark articles as “Required Reading”. Alt Text: Screenshot of the Manage News app with the toggles set to “Yes”, and some news text in the Description field.

 

News and Pages visibility - A more focused experience 

In the 2602 release we introduced enhanced customization options, allowing business users to control the visibility of news and pages within My Home. This enables users to tailor their workspace according to their role, priorities, and daily responsibilities. By showing only relevant information and tools, My Home becomes more focused and clutter-free, helping users stay productive and work more efficiently without unnecessary distractions. 

My Home with enhanced personalization, showing News deselected and Pages selected. Alt Text: A screenshot of My Home, with eight cards in one row in the Pages section, below the ToDos section showing one situation and the Apps section.

 

My Home Settings pop-over with the new selectors for News and Pages within the News and Pages section. Alt Text: A screenshot showing the Layout part of My Home Settings, where users can select or deselect sections. For the News and Pages section, News is deselected, Pages are selected.

 

Improved Mobile Experience with horizontal scrolling 

For business users on the move, the My Home mobile experience has been enhanced with horizontal scrolling. This update supports visual browsing and improves the discoverability of tasks, apps, and insight cards on smaller screens. As a result, mobile users can navigate content more intuitively and access important information quickly—wherever they are. 

 

AI-assisted Content Addition - Adding apps using natural language. 

My Home enables you to do your job with speed and agility. Even more so now that our 2508 Beta AI – Assisted content addition feature is now available in our 2602 release as a premium feature.  Business users can now add applications using natural language in two clicks. The business user can review and verify the results generated by AI ensuring accuracy.  This saves time by dramatically simplifying the process of adding new applications and tiles to My Home. Now with this new AI assisted feature,  there is no longer a need for business users to discover the appropriate application or even to navigate away from their home page to the application and manually apply filters to find the information they require — thanks to Generative AI, the relevant information can be surfaced quickly and effortlessly using natural language. This significantly reduces the effort needed to discover and add Insights Cards. 

AI-Assisted Smart Personalization of My Home for Applications. Alt Text: The image shows three screenshots: at the top an extract of My Home showing the blue salutation bar, with an AI button “Add Content” on the right. An arrow has been added linking it to the second screenshot, where a popover “Add Content” is shown, where the user has entered “I want to monitor sales orders what app should I use to do this”, and two of four results are listed and visualized: “Track Sales Orders” and “Manage Sales Orders” (shown as an insight tile). Below that a screenshot of the outcome in My Home: the former is added to Apps Favorites, the latter to Insights Tiles.

 

Smart Helpers - Automating Repetitive Tasks 

Business users now have access to the Beta  AI – assisted task automation in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 release. It allows key users and business users to automate their daily activities in SAP’s Fiori apps by using generative AI to create so call “smart helpers”. Those “smart helpers” execute the repetitive activities on behalf of the business user either from the SAP Fiori apps while always keeping the business user in the loop for decisions on data changes.  Business users simply describe their desired business outcome in natural language, related to a specific SAP Fiori elements-based application, and the smart helper will take care of it for you. 

Beta: The planned way of giving users an overview of the AI-assisted task automation smart helpers in My Home. Alt Text: Screenshot of My Home with a section “Smart Helpers” above the “Apps” section, with two tabs “Smart Helpers (4)” and “Active Runs (3)”. The latter is selected, showing three cards with title, description and executed step shown: one is completed, two are running.

 

Beta: Manage Smart Helpers application for defining instructions for the planned AI-assisted task automation. Alt Text: Screenshot showing the instruction text at the top, an empty section for attachments, and the execution plan with four steps: read order requests, analyze request data, simulate pending requests, create sales orders.

 

AI-assisted Smart Solution for situations – Simplifying workflow 

The Beta AI-assisted Smart Solution for Situations in My Home simplifies workflows by dynamically adapting the user interface to guide business users through resolution steps using the power of generative AI. Key information and actions are surfaced though dynamic cards reducing the need for in-depth navigation.  One click resolution of situations directly from the homepage thereby enhancing efficiency. There is support for bank messages for alerts, reaching project end dates when a project stage is not completed and sales quotation expiry situations. 

Beta: AI-Assisted Smart Solution for Situations in My Home. Alt Text: An image with two screenshots: the upper one show the top part of My Home showing five Situation cards in the To-Dos section. One titled “End Date for Project Digital Media Assessment for Media Corp…” is linked with an arrow to an expanded card shown in the second screenshot – the card is taller and wider, contains a summary on the left with two action buttons “Assign To Me” and “Close Situation”, and more details on the right.

 

Final Thoughts 

The My Home 2602 release demonstrates our continued focus on usability, personalization, and effective communication. From mandatory news articles that drive accountability to enhanced customization and mobile usability, these improvements help ensure that My Home remains a powerful and user-centric entry point to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 Beta AI-assisted features such as Beta AI-assisted Smart solution for situations and Smart Helpers are now available for Beta Testing with the 2602 release. At SAP, we’re committed to delivering the best experience possible, which is why we’re inviting you to join our Beta Testing Program. By participating, you’ll get early access to these features, have the chance to share your feedback, and help us fine-tune them before the official launch. Your input will play a key role in shaping the quality and usability of these new capabilities.  

Also, If you are interested in hearing about the latest innovations in SAP Cloud ERP, please join our Public Cloud Practitioner Forum in which SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition customers share best practices and provide feedback to the SAP development teams about new functionality.

entry.title: Inbound, Outbound, Certificate Bound: Certificate Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Certificates are key to secure integrations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, but their roles often cause confusion. This post clarifies the different certificate types and shows how to manage them using three key SAP Fiori apps.

Introduction

Navigating certificate terms like TLS, client authentication, and certificate mapping can be a challenge at first. Fortunately, understanding their usage in S/4HANA Cloud makes it easier to secure connections and authenticate users or systems. We'll cover:

  • Certificates in S/4HANA Cloud: their purpose and types
  • How S/4HANA Cloud acts as both client and server
  • Which SAP Fiori apps to use to manage certificates

Certificate Basics

For a detailed explanation of what certificates are and how they work, see this blog post: Beyond Basic (1): Certificate-Based Authentication. Here, we just recap the main usage types.

TLS Server Certificates: Know Who You Called

TLS (Transport Layer Security) is a widely used internet standard for encrypting and validating connections between systems. It’s not unique to S/4HANA Cloud—almost all web communications rely on TLS for encryption and to confirm that the client is connecting to the intended server.
When a client initiates a connection, it checks the remote server’s TLS certificate against a list of trusted certificates (more specifically: it checks the certificate and compares the issuer of the certificate against a list of trusted issuers).

Client Certificates: Know Who Called You

Once the connection is secured by TLS and the client has verified the server’s identity, the next step is for the server to confirm that the client is authorized to connect. This authentication process works similarly to traditional user/password logins, but instead of a password, the client presents a certificate. This certificate-based method is a more secure way for the server to verify the client’s identity.

Client and Server Roles in Communication

In any communication, there’s a client (the caller) and a server (the responder). The client always checks the server’s identity via a TLS certificate, and the server can verify the client’s identity through a client certificate.

S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition can act as either client or server, depending on whether it’s making outbound connections or accepting inbound ones. Depending on the role the S/4 system takes, it either needs to present a client certificate to authenticate itself to the remote server (when acting as a client), or verify the client certificate of the incoming call (when acting as a server).

Three Maintenance Apps for Certificates

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, there are three key Fiori apps to manage certificates:

  • Maintain Certificate Trust List: List and manage trusted certificate authorities (CAs) for outbound server validation
  • Maintain Client Certificates: Store and export certificates for outbound client authentication
  • Maintain Communication Users: Map inbound certificates to communication users for secure access

The maintained certificates are then referenced in the Communication Systems app and the Communication Arrangements app.

Outbound Server Validation (TLS server certificates)

When S/4HANA Cloud connects outbound, it validates the remote server’s certificate against the Certificate Trust List. The Manage Certificate Trust List app lets you add and manage trusted Certificate Authorities (CAs)—the issuing authorities of the server’s certificate.
Good to know: every SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition tenant comes with an SAP-managed list of trusted CAs (similar to the pre-delivered trust list in your browser). So most of the time, you don’t have to do much here.

As explained above, this TLS server validation is not related to authenticating specific users; it’s simply a prerequisite for establishing a secure connection.

Outbound Client Authentication

When S/4HANA Cloud connects to another system as a client, it may present a certificate for authentication. These certificates are stored in the Maintain Client Certificates app.
Good to know: every SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition tenant comes with an SAP-managed client certificate preinstalled. But you can of course create your own.

The specific certificate to be used for a connection is then referenced in the Communication Systems app under “Users for Outbound Communication” and is then selected in the communication arrangement for the specific outbound integration.

Inbound Certificate Mapping

External systems connecting to S/4HANA Cloud present their certificates, which are mapped to communication users. The user mapping happens in the Maintain Communication Users app. There you upload the client certificate (just the public key!) of the caller. This maps the communication user to the subject and issuer attributes of the client certificate.

Important to know:

  • the client's certificate is not “pinned”, so rotating the certificate is possible without re-upload (unless the issuer or subject change, of course).
  • only client certificates signed by specific CAs are accepted by SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. The list of these CAs cannot be changed but is predetermined by SAP and available in SAP Note 2801396.  In particular: this list is not the one maintained in the Manage Certificate Trust List app.

Conclusion

Grasping the various certificate types and their roles is crucial, though it can be complex. The main factor in successfully handling certificates is understanding your communication scenario—whether your system operates as a client or a server. This knowledge guides you in choosing the appropriate app for configuring the correct settings, ensuring your communication environment remains secure.

entry.title: GRC, Trade and Tax in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I'm excited to share the update regarding our latest release - GRC, Trade and Tax in SAP Cloud ERP 2602. This update highlights many significant innovations from SAP Risk and Assurance Management, SAP Watch List Screening, SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, as well as SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance.

For a quick overview of the top release highlights for GRC with 2602, you can watch the video below.

Video 1. Release Highlight Video for GRC, Trade and Tax in SAP Cloud ERP 2602

This blog covers the following innovations:

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

  • AI-assisted trend analysis and summary for reports (beta)
  • Embedded analytics to facilitate VAT adjustments
  • E- verify and Automated Handling of Draft VAT Returns from Authorities
  • Enabling Electronic Invoices Through KSeF 2.0 for Poland​

SAP Risk and Assurance Management

  • Extended analytical and reporting capabilities: new reporting dashboards with SAP Analytics Cloud, enterprise edition

SAP Enterprise Threat Detection

  • Intelligent alert handling and investigation recommendations
  • Management-focused monitoring dashboard

SAP International Trade

  • Export declaration for plants abroad in the context of Registration for Indirect Taxation Abroad (RITA)

​SAP Watchlist Screening

  • Delta Screening​

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

AI-assisted trend analysis and report summary

Tax accountants often spend significant time validating data by comparing current tax figures with previous returns. To accelerate this process, we introduce AI-assisted trend analysis for VAT returns. This feature helps tax experts by automatically uncovering hidden trends, flagging outliers, and detecting root causes for discrepancies. It provides intelligent summaries that can be used to simplify the approval process and support future audits. This innovation allows the team to move from reactive validation to proactive analysis.

Picture 1. AI-assisted trend analysis and report summary

Value Proposition​
  • Reduce the time needed to interpret VAT data across periods, leading to faster reviews, quicker approvals, and shorter reporting cycles.
  • Accuracy is improved and compliance risk is reduced. This helps identify anomalies early, minimizing manual errors and lowering the risk of incorrect filings.
  • Easy to make clear and informative data-driven decisions.
Capabilities
  • The analysis highlights key trends, fluctuations, and irregularities, and flags values that help to identify potential errors or compliance risks. ​
  • Provide concise summary of key VAT insights to support faster reviews, approvals, and decision-making.​

Embedded analytics to facilitate VAT adjustments

During the preparation of statutory reports, tax experts require deep visibility into their data to ensure accuracy. With the 2602 release, the 'Manage Tax Items' activity within SAP Document and Reporting Compliance is enhanced with a powerful ''analyze data" option.

This provides tax experts with additional fields and the flexibility to analyze tax data to identify anomalies. Now you can easily see how invoices are mapped to your VAT returns and pinpoint any documents that might have been excluded, enabling seamless adjustments integrated directly with accounting. To further tailor the solution, you can now use extensibility to plug in your own custom analytics CDS views, allowing you to choose between standard SAP-delivered queries or your own defined views for analysis.

 

Video 2. Embedded analytics to facilitate VAT adjustments

Value Proposition​
  • Advances the VAT review and adjustment by integrating analytics.
  • Improves accuracy and strengthens compliance through data-driven decisions.
  • Reduces manual effort and reliance on offline analysis and external tools​.
  • Enhances analytical transparency and enables faster and more confident decision-making.​
Capabilities ​
  • Embedded analytics within the Manage Tax Items for Legal Reporting application​
  • Direct access to detailed VAT tax line-item records with added data points​
  • Interactive analytical views with charts, filters, and drill-down capabilities​
  • Trend analysis and comparisons across multiple VAT dimensions​
  • Identification of documents for inclusion in or exclusion from VAT returns​
  • Analysis at the point of action without switching applications​
  • Support for efficient VAT reconciliation and compliance preparation​

For more information, see: Manage Tax Items for Legal Reporting | SAP Help Portal

E- verify and Automated Handling of Draft VAT Returns from Authorities

A growing number of tax authorities are providing pre-populated, or draft, tax returns based on real-time transactional data. Our latest update helps you automate the handling of these draft returns, ensuring consistency with your company's books. With this feature, you can pull auto-populated returns from an authority's portal and automatically compare them with the returns prepared in your ERP system to identify any mismatches.

Based on the reconciliation results, you can then process actions such as submitting a delta file, accepting, or rejecting the draft. This streamlines the entire process, from review and correction to the submission of the final return.

 

Video 3. E- verify and Automated Handling of Draft VAT Returns from Authorities

Enabling electronic invoices through KSeF 2.0 for Poland

With the Polish Ministry of Finance enforcing the use of electronic invoices through the KSeF 2.0 platform beginning in February 2026, we are equipping you to meet this mandate head-on. SAP Document and Reporting Compliance now enables you to generate B2B e-invoices in the required XML format from both Finance (FI) and Sales & Distribution (SD), including tax invoices, correction invoices, down-payment invoices, and self-billing invoices. You can submit these invoices interactively to KSeF 2.0, visualize them in a human-readable PDF format with a QR code, and retrieve supplier invoices for automated posting.

Picture 2. Enabling electronic invoices through KSeF 2.0 for Poland

SAP Risk and Assurance Management

Extended analytical and reporting capabilities: new reporting dashboards with SAP Analytics Cloud, enterprise edition

We are introducing the new Risk and Compliance Cockpit, an SAP Analytics Cloud story that gives you an instant, comprehensive overview of your organization's compliance landscape.

This dashboard empowers compliance managers to identify process inefficiencies by visualizing compliance issues organized by type and status, with charts showing trends over time. It makes it easy to compare current performance against historical data, highlight long-term trends, and identify deviations. With insights into average issue resolution times and year-to-year analysis, this single source of truth helps you prioritize critical unresolved issues and improve operational efficiency.

 

Video 4.  Extended analytical and reporting capabilities: new reporting dashboards with SAP Analytics Cloud, enterprise edition

Value Proposition​
  • Get an instant overview of compliance issues, organized by type and status​
  • Improved operational efficiency supported by trend and year-to-year analysis​
  • Charts visualize trends over time​
  • Overview of what to prioritize by highlighting critical unresolved issues​
​Capabilities ​
  • Identify inefficiencies in organizations' processes​
  • Instant overview of compliance issues​
  • Filter business data to examine specific categories of compliance issues​
  • Visual and comparative analysis of data​
  • Compare current performance while highlighting long-term trends and deviations

For more information, see: Risk and Compliance Cockpit

SAP Enterprise Threat Detection

Intelligent alert handling and investigation recommendations

In SAP Enterprise Threat Detection, cloud edition, investigations are the central hub for examining security incidents. This new capability automates and enhances this critical process.

The primary value of this feature is to decrease the manual effort required by SAP monitoring agents and increase their overall efficiency. When an agent prepares an investigation to send to a customer, they are now supported with automatic, case-specific response and mitigation recommendations.

This helps teams by automatically compiling investigation results and formulating suggestions for customers on how to address the alerts. The system intelligently generates these recommendations based on the standard operating procedure of the specific pattern that created the alert, further enhanced by predefined value lists and machine learning functionality. This ensures that the advice provided is both highly relevant and immediately actionable, allowing your security teams to move from detection to resolution faster and more effectively.

Picture 3. Intelligent alert handling and investigation recommendations

For more information, see: Generating Investigation Recommendations

Management-focused monitoring dashboard

We are introducing a powerful new monitoring dashboard within SAP Enterprise Threat Detection, cloud edition, specifically designed for management roles. In today's complex security landscape, having a clear, high-level view of your organization's cyberhealth is not just an advantage; it is a necessity. This new dashboard is for all managers and security leaders who need to quickly understand current security events, alerts, and ongoing investigations to make informed, timely decisions.

It facilitates a deeper understanding of complex security data by offering a comprehensive picture of your system's cyberhealth and protection status. The dashboard also shows the distribution of security events by various criteria, effectively highlighting the most affected systems, patterns, and users to help you focus resources where they are needed most.

To ensure the information is tailored to your specific needs, you can also create your own personalized monitoring pages with a selection of charts that are most relevant to your responsibilities..

Picture 4. Management-focused monitoring dashboard

For more information, see: Using the Monitoring Dashboard

SAP International Trade

Export declaration for plants abroad in the context of Registration for Indirect Taxation Abroad (RITA)

For businesses operating with plants abroad, ensuring the correct export declaration is crucial. In a plant abroad scenario, this feature ensures that the country of the delivering plant, rather than the country of the company code, determines the correct foreign trade organization for the customs declaration. This enhancement improves compliance with international trade regulations for businesses leveraging the "Registration for Indirect Taxation Abroad" (RITA) scope.

Value Proposition
  • Enhance compliance with trade regulations by ensuring the correct foreign trade organization is used for export declarations.
Capabilities 
  • Manage your export declaration in SAP Global Trade Services using the foreign trade organization within the country of the plant abroad.
  • Identify plants abroad on pro forma invoice line items by considering countries where registration for indirect taxation (RITA) is enabled.

In SAP Cloud ERP,  a billing document exists where goods delivery is performed via a foreign plant, for example, the plant country is different from the country of the company code.

Picture 5. Export declaration for plants abroad in the context of Registration for Indirect Taxation Abroad (RITA)

In SAP GTS, Edition for SAP HANA, the FTO of the corresponding export declaration is now determined based on the legal unit corresponding to the foreign plant used in the billing document.

Picture 6. Export declaration for plants abroad in the context of Registration for Indirect Taxation Abroad (RITA)

For more information, see: Enablement of Plants-Abroad for Export Processing and Foreign Plant​

Delta screening with SAP Watch List Screening and manual release of blocked document items

Continuous compliance with sanction party lists is critical. Screening requests sent to SAP Watch List Screening will now remain active for delta screening for up to one month. This means the system automatically performs delta screenings against any changes to watch lists and updates the status of your business partners and documents accordingly, providing automatic coverage without requiring new manual screenings.

Additionally, for situations requiring manual intervention, the "Manage Documents - Trade Compliance" Fiori app now allows authorized users to manually release a trade compliance document item that is blocked. This creates a simple, straightforward, and auditable overrule process to comply with legal requirements while ensuring business continuity.

Picture 7. Delta screening with SAP Watch List Screening and manual release of blocked document items

For more information, see: Integration with SAP Watch List Screening and Delta Screening in SAP Watch List Screening

Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series! 

From January 12-16, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find the recording and presentation for Artificial Intelligence as well as 21 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime.

Find all our assets 

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below: 

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on Hiruni Arunodhi Badulle | LinkedIn.

entry.title: Core Logistics in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
Welcome to the 2602 release of SAP Cloud ERP where we have introduced a set of enhancements focused on strengthening day-to-day supply chain execution and planning. These updates help streamline key logistics activities and improve user experience, so teams can execute faster, improve material visibility, and make more reliable, data-driven decisions across the end-to-end logistics flow.  In this blog post, you'll find an overview of the key new innovations and highlight features being rolled out with this release.

Short on time? The below video gives a summary of the top 3 highlights from this release.


 

 



Inventory Management

In the inventory management area, new features and apps have bene introduced to improve efficiency and accuracy when managing and moving physical inventory.

Streamlining Physical Inventory Management with new app "Manage Physical Inventory Item List" 

Managing physical inventory is about far more than counting stock: it’s about ensuring every item is tracked, verified, and processed correctly across potentially hundreds or thousands of line items, multiple locations, and multiple documents. In this kind of environment, speed and accuracy are critical. With this in mind, in the 2602 release of SAP Cloud ERP, SAP introduces the new Fiori app Manage Physical Inventory Item List (F8634)—giving inventory managers one clear, powerful place to manage physical inventory items across all relevant documents. The new app is designed to bring clarity and structure to this time-sensitive process by offering intuitive, user-friendly list reports and comprehensive status visibility so users can immediately see what needs attention and what’s already complete.

When you open the app, you start with flexible filtering options that let you tailor the list to the exact scenario you’re working on—such as narrowing down by plant and storage location to generate an overview for a specific area. Filters can be expanded easily, making it simple to adapt the screen to different use cases. From the resulting list, you can select items for further processing and execute key follow-up actions directly in the same place—including posting, triggering a recount, or printing—without jumping between applications. This is where the app really shines: it enables managers to filter physical inventory items by processing status (for example, showing only Counted items) so they can focus on the next required step and move work forward efficiently across multiple inventory documents.

A typical end-to-end scenario highlights the productivity gains. After physical inventory documents have been created and warehouse staff have completed counting, the inventory manager can filter by planned count date and then refine the list by status—such as showing only counted items. From there, it’s easy to review count details, identify quantity differences, and decide the right follow-up action. Items with no difference can be mass-selected and posted in just a click. Items with large discrepancies can be selected and sent for recount, where the user defines a recount date and (if needed) adds header information—automatically generating a new physical inventory document so warehouse teams know exactly what to recount. For marginal differences, the manager can still proceed with posting based on business rules, and if the system is configured with reason codes, the app prompts users to select the appropriate code during posting—supporting compliant, auditable processing aligned with company requirements. Finally, managers can confirm outcomes by filtering for posted items and, when needed, navigate directly from the list to the created material documents for review and further processing.

Overall, Manage Physical Inventory Item List (F8634) increases inventory transparency and helps teams complete inventory processing faster and with fewer errors. By combining comprehensive list reporting, efficient cross-document processing, and status-driven filtering in a single, streamlined experience, the app supports inventory managers with the clarity they need to keep physical inventory moving from count to resolution—accurately, quickly, and with full control.

 

 

Supporting Intra-Plant Stock Transfer Reservations

In many plants, materials have to be moved between storage locations to keep production and operations running—often at short notice and under time pressure. While these movements are typically planned using reservations (created manually or generated by MRP), execution has historically required warehouse staff to switch between apps or rely on classic processing such as MIGO—adding effort and increasing the risk of mistakes. With this new enhancement, intra-plant stock transfers become significantly easier and faster: warehouse teams can now use the Manage Reservation Items app to post stock transfers directly from a reservation for both manual and MRP-generated reservations using movement type 311, all in one place.

In practice, users filter and review the relevant reservation items, select multiple lines at once, and create the goods movement as a single, streamlined action—completing any missing details as needed before posting. Once posted, the system automatically creates two material document entries—one for the goods issue and one for the goods receipt—so the transfer is fully captured and traceable without extra steps. The result is clear business value: mass posting of several reservation items in one go, improved usability through integrated goods movement posting, and more time-efficient processing for busy warehouse teams.

 

Monitor Material Documents More Effectively : Material Documents Overview Enhancements 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP’s Material Documents Overview app (F1077) is a modern SAP Fiori replacement for the classic MB51 experience—built to help you review and analyze goods movements with a smoother, more intuitive UX while closing remaining functional gaps step by step. In one place, users can search, filter, sort, and drill into material documents to understand what moved, when it posted, and how it impacts operations and stock. This transition is more than “nice to have”: moving to the new app is essential because legacy options will ultimately be deprecated, and SAP is actively enhancing F1077 based on real customer feedback to make switching worthwhile.

Driven directly by customer input, SAP has been evolving the app’s functional scope and usability to better match how supply chain and warehouse teams actually work. Recent enhancements include the availability of additional fields—notably amount in local currency and sales value—to make financial and commercial context visible alongside logistics data. The app also now supports signed quantity, reducing the mental gymnastics of interpreting directionality across receipts and issues. And for users who regularly pivot between quantity-focused and value-focused analysis, SAP introduced a variant that displays amounts and values, making it easier to switch perspectives and tailor the overview to different roles and reporting needs.

In the 2602 release, the app takes a more visual and analytical leap forward with two planned improvements that make high-volume review work noticeably smoother. First, color coding for selected quantities (such as Signed Quantity)helps users identify the right figures at a glance, reducing errors and speeding up interpretation during daily checks. Second, sums for quantities and values add instant rollups for quick validation and comparison—particularly useful when reconciling multiple postings or checking totals across filtered selections. Together, these 2602 features continue the push to improve usability while enhancing the functional scope in the goods movement process, keeping the Material Documents Overview app firmly positioned as a practical tool for operational control in SAP Cloud ERP.

For further details, check out this blog by @stefanie_bierbach 

 


Handling Unit Management

Effective Handling Unit Management is fundamental to an integrated and transparent supply chain. Our latest updates to SAP Cloud ERP introduce significant enhancements in this area, designed to increase operational flexibility, improve system integration, and ensure alignment with global standards

Terminology Alignment with Global Standards for SSCC

To promote clarity and ensure consistency with global industry practices, we have updated the terminology used in the configuration activities for Serial Shipping Container Code (SSCC) numbering. The term "ILN basis number" has been replaced with "GS1 company prefix." This alignment with current GS1 standards helps reduce ambiguity during system setup and ensures your organizations handling unit identification is compliant with international norms. This change enhances usability for global enterprises without altering any underlying system logic.

Interface Support for Empty Handling Unit Creation

To provide greater flexibility for integration scenarios, the OData API for Handling Unit Management now supports the creation of empty handling units. This enhancement enables external systems or custom applications to first create a handling unit container and then subsequently manage the packing of materials into it. This capability allows organizations to model real-world logistics processes more accurately, where containers are often prepared before their contents are finalized. This facilitates more sophisticated and responsive integration with warehouse automation systems and third-party logistics platforms.

 

New Business Event for Handling Unit Creation

We are introducing a new business event that is triggered upon the creation of a handling unit within an Inventory Management-managed storage location. This event-driven mechanism enables real-time communication with external systems, such as parcel service providers or transportation management platforms, instantly notifying them when a handling unit is ready. This capability allows organizations to automate downstream processes, improve the timeliness of data exchange, and enhance end-to-end visibility, thereby reducing manual coordination and improving overall supply chain responsiveness.

 

Integration with External Output Management Systems

This release introduces a new feature that allows for the integration of Handling Unit Management with external, best-of-breed output management systems. By enabling the external channel in your output parameter determination, you can now send raw data for handling unit labels and documents directly to a preferred third-party solution. This provides organizations with maximum flexibility to design complex form layouts, manage specialized printing hardware, and utilize advanced output channels. The external system processes the output and sends a status confirmation back to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, ensuring full process traceability and control.

 

 

Further Services to Support Partial Goods Receipt for Inbound Delivery with Third-Party Logistics Warehouse Management (Release 2508.2) 

We are excited to introduce a significant enhancement to our warehouse logistics capabilities: the ability to post partial goods receipts for inbound deliveries from your third-party Warehouse Management System (WMS). This feature directly addresses a common bottleneck at the receiving dock by empowering your warehouse staff to begin the putaway process immediately after unloading parts of a delivery, rather than waiting to check the entire shipment. This new flexibility accommodates a wide range of scenarios, allowing you to process complete or fractional quantities for single or multiple delivery items, both with and without handling units. This ultimately accelerates inventory availability and dramatically improves warehouse throughput.

To ensure seamless data integrity, this process is supported by robust, automated communication between SAP S/4HANA Cloud and your WMS. For every partial receipt posted, a confirmation is sent to the WMS, and a straightforward process allows for the cancellation of any erroneous postings. Once all parts of the delivery have been received, the inbound delivery in SAP S/4HANA Cloud is automatically updated to reflect the final, accurate totals for quantity, weight, and volume. A final confirmation is then sent to the WMS, guaranteeing that your core ERP system is perfectly synchronized with the physical reality documented by your warehouse partner.

📽️ Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series!

From January 12-16, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find the recording and presentation for Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management as well as 21 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand – anywhere, anytime.

 

🔎Find all our assets

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: What's New in Supply Chain in SAP Cloud ERP 2602?
entry.content:
In this blog, we're excited to introduce you to some of the most impactful updates for Supply Chain in the Cloud ERP 2602 release. This update is all about empowering you with greater visibility, streamlined processes, and enhanced flexibility across your entire supply chain. Whether you work in inventory, warehousing, or transportation, these new features are designed to make your daily work more efficient and intelligent. Below, you'll find a summary of the main highlights from each area of our supply chain solution, and if you follow the links, you'll find a comprehensive overview of further new features in each area from our expert product managers.


2602 Release Highlights Overview

To kick things off, we've put together an overview of our top  supply chain highlights in the 2602 release of SAP Cloud ERP.

 

Warehouse Management in SAP Cloud ERP 2602

One fresh Warehouse Management feature we're keen to highlight is the new Joule capability to assist with your Warehouse Management Operations, which we'll look at below. Of course, there's much more to come -  make sure to check out @Christian_Ries blog for the full scope of release highlights in Warehouse Management in the 2602 release. 

Joule Enablement For Outbound Delivery Orders & Warehouse Tasks

Building on recent enhancements, we continue to expand the capabilities of Joule, our generative AI assistant. Now, Joule can assist users in accessing and managing key business objects in Warehouse Management using natural language. You can use simple conversational language to process delivery orders and warehouse tasks, ask questions, display data, and receive navigation links to relevant applications. Joule can also perform actions such as creating and updating business objects. This functionality simplifies user interaction and empowers your team to control warehouse operations with greater efficiency and accuracy, directly through a natural language interface.

 

Automatic Packing for Direct Goods Receipt Posting after Production Order Confirmation 

A recent key enhancement automates the packing of goods following production. Previously, a direct goods receipt could be posted automatically upon confirmation of a production order. Now, this process has been enhanced to include automatic packing. The system can be configured to automatically pack any unpacked products into handling units based on predefined packaging instructions. This action is triggered during the confirmation of a manufacturing order, eliminating the need for manual user input. This feature increases efficiency on the production floor by combining automatable steps and improves accuracy by providing total automation from confirmation to packed stock, which reduces the potential for human error.

 

Transportation Management in SAP Cloud ERP 2602

In the below demo, @Markus_Oertelt talks you through the complete transportation lifecycle associated with subcontracting purchase orders. This seamless integration is released in 2602 and enables transportation planning, execution and freight settlement via subcontractors with minimal manual effort and reduced scope for errors. Of course, that's not all, and for an overview of further TM highlights in this release, check this blog out from TM Expert @thomas_roesch.

 

Enable Subcontracting Purchase Order Integration With Transportation Management

This powerful new feature enables seamless integration of subcontracting purchase orders with Transportation Management (TM). Users can now manage the complete transportation lifecycle associated with the subcontracting process, from planning transportation demand based on purchase order requirements to executing movements and settling freight costs for both outbound and inbound processes. For outbound processes, transportation demand from the delivery to the subcontractor can be planned, executed, and settled. For inbound processes, the same applies to the delivery of the finished product. This integration streamlines the end-to-end process, reduces manual effort, ensures accurate cost allocation, and provides real-time visibility, allowing for greater control and transparency over subcontracting logistics.

 


Core Logistics in SAP Cloud ERP 2602

Under core logistics, we include the backbone of any well-oiled supply chain solution, featuring inventory management, delivery management, handling-unit & serial number management. With release 2602, we're particularly excited by the improved user experience when managing physical inventory delivered by the new app Manage Physical Inventory Items List. Of course, that's not all, and for some more core logistics highlights, look no further than the overview blog by @Greg_Hutcheon1. 

Introducing Manage Physical Inventory Item List (App F8634)


In the 2602 release of SAP Cloud ERP, SAP introduces the new Fiori app Manage Physical Inventory Item List (F8634) to help inventory managers process physical inventory faster and with greater accuracy. Instead of switching between multiple documents and apps, users get a single, structured list report with clear status visibility across all relevant physical inventory items, making it easy to see what’s pending, in progress, or complete.

The app offers flexible filters (for example by plant, storage location, planned count date, and processing status) so managers can quickly focus on the right subset of items—such as “Counted” entries ready for follow-up. From the same screen, they can execute key actions like posting, triggering recounts, or printing, including mass processing across multiple inventory documents. In a typical flow, managers review counted items, identify differences, post items with no variance in one step, and initiate recounts for major discrepancies by setting a recount date and generating a new physical inventory document. If configured, the app also supports compliant processing by prompting for reason codes during posting. Overall, F8634 improves transparency, reduces errors, and streamlines inventory processing from count to resolution.

 


Order Promising

New BAdI for Confirmation Acceptance Rule

In this release, SAP Cloud ERP added a powerful new extension point for Order Promising: the Confirmation Adjustment (Confirmation Acceptance) BAdI, also known as “adjust ATP confirmation.” It lets you take the standard confirmations calculated by SAP ATP—quantities and dates—then apply your own business logic on top after the basic ATP check.

With this BAdI, you can override confirmed quantities or shift confirmation dates to better match business priorities (like customer tiers, allocation rules, or operational constraints). The adjusted confirmation can be written back and persisted in the relevant tables, so downstream processes work with the business-approved result—not just the original ATP output.

To keep things safe and explainable, SAP includes validations to prevent over-confirmation, and the system records the custom outcome in the ATP check log for full transparency. The adjusted values can also influence BOP decisions(for example via FIXMG), giving you more control over how confirmations behave during backorder processing.

Supply Protection Indicator in the Display Product Availability App

The Display Product Availability app now includes an easy-to-understand Supply Protection (SUP) indicator with two states (on/off). It adds immediate context to the available quantity by showing whether that quantity may be subject to supply protection.

Note: The icon is shown as active when supply protection is enabled for the specific product in the specific plant.

A SUP icon now appears directly next to a product’s available quantity. It indicates whether Supply Protection is considered in the availability calculation, helping users quickly interpret what they’re seeing without extra checks. This improves day-to-day decision-making by giving users instant visibility into whether the displayed quantity is “straight availability” or potentially restricted by supply protection—saving time and reducing confusion during availability checks.

 

Summary

This blog provides an overview of the key features and functions delivered in the 2602 release of SAP Cloud ERP. Make sure to follow the links above to find about more supply chain features in depth with the relevant specialist blogs. Beyond supply chain capabilities, the 2602 SAP Cloud ERP release was packed full of great new features designed to drive success. For an overview of the key highlights from this release, make sure to check out the 2602 release blog by @BeSchulze  

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Click on the enablement wheel below and check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you:

entry.title: How SAP ESM + Document AI + S/4HANA Work Together to Automate Invoice Processing End‑to‑End
entry.content:
One of the most powerful capabilities in SAP Enterprise Service Management (ESM) is its ability to automate invoice processing the moment a customer or vendor sends an email. With SAP Document Information Extraction (Document AI) integrated into ESM, the journey from a raw invoice PDF to a validated posting in SAP S/4HANA Private or Public Edition becomes almost fully automated.
Click on Image to view High Quality
Click on Image to view High Quality

Step 1: The User Sends an Email with an Invoice

A customer or vendor sends an email to the SAP ESM dedicated email address configured for automatic case creation.

The invoice may be:

  • Attached as a PDF, JPEG, PNG, or
  • Embedded in the email body itself

SAP ESM picks up both.

Step 2: SAP ESM Automatically Creates a Case

Once the email reaches the ESM mailbox:

  • A new case is created automatically
  • The email body and all attachments are stored within the case
  • The attachments (PDFs, images) trigger SAP Document AI processing immediately

This eliminates manual case creation entirely.

Step 3: SAP Document AI Processes the Invoice

SAP Document AI retrieves the attachment from the case and begins extraction using OCR and machine learning.

It identifies:

  • Invoice number
  • Customer or vendor name
  • Address
  • Line items
  • Totals & taxes
  • Payment terms
  • Contract or PO references

Document AI also calculates a confidence score for each extracted field.
These scores help determine whether the data is reliable enough to auto‑post or requires user intervention.

Step 4: Validation Using Business Rules & Customer Framework

Once extraction is complete, SAP ESM applies:

  • Business rules
  • Customer validation frameworks
  • Mapping logic
  • Master data checks

These validations are essential before communicating with S/4HANA.

Examples:

  • Is the customer known in S/4?
  • Is the invoice number valid and not duplicated?
  • Does the PO exist?
  • Do line item totals match?

Only when the validations pass does ESM proceed to the next step.

Step 5: Invoice Number & Customer Auto‑Identification

If Document AI finds the invoice number, SAP ESM updates the line items and case fields:

  • invoiceNumber → updated
  • customerNumber → auto‑identified
  • isClearing → set according to logic (e.g., invoice found, ready to post)

This gives the ESM user immediate visibility:

  • Has the system recognized the invoice?
  • Has the customer been identified?
  • Is the invoice ready for posting?

Step 6: ESM Sends the Validated Invoice to S/4HANA via OData APIs

Once the case has all mandatory and validated data, the ESM user can trigger the next action:

“Post Invoice to S/4”

At this point:

  • ESM calls S/4HANA OData APIs
  • A draft or final FI invoice is created
  • Additional steps like clearing, matching, or workflow approvals occur inside S/4

This hands-off process ensures:

  • No manual entry in S/4
  • No data inconsistencies
  • Full auditability from source → document → posting

Step 7: ESM User Monitors and Completes the Process

Inside the ESM case, the user can now see:

  • Status of the posting
  • Document number returned from S/4
  • Any errors (e.g., master data inconsistencies)
  • New actions available for follow-up

They can then:

  • Update the customer
  • Add missing reference information
  • Re-trigger the posting
  • Close the case once processing is complete

Result: A Fully Streamlined, AI‑Driven Invoice Workflow

By integrating SAP ESM, Document AI, and S/4HANA, organizations benefit from:

✔ Zero manual invoice entry

✔ Auto-identification of customers and invoice data

✔ Early error detection using business rules

✔ Faster posting to S/4HANA

✔ End-to-end transparency in one central case

✔ Higher accuracy and reduced effort

This modernizes invoice handling from a traditionally manual process into an intelligent, automated workflow.

Learning Journey :  Business Documents in ESM

entry.title: Budget Management in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello SAP Folks,

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, Budget Management (known as Funds Management in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud) has been revamped and its structure has been changed. Following is a brief introduction to Budget Management in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Introduction to Budget Management in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud:

Budget management supports budgeting-based management of funds at public sector institutions where revenue and expenditure are budgeted according to legislative criteria, such as payment in kind, area of responsibility, program/function and source of funds. All the financial transactions which leads to eventual outflow of cash are monitored strictly via an allocated budget and revenues planning and monitoring is also done. Planning, posting, maintenance of budget, monitoring is all possible if we have Budget Management in place .

The business benefits of using Budget Management are as follows:

  • Maintain and monitor the allocation, distribution, or changes of approved budgets
  • Plan, standardize, schedule, and monitor financial and budget close activities
  • Automate and streamline budget processes, and reduce manual effort
  • Optimize closing of open commitments and financial and managerial accounting
  • Immediate budget availability checking to ensure regulatory compliance
  • Real-time reporting to aid in time-sensitive managerial decision

A helpful link is given below which has helped me a lot to demystify the Budget management and its underlying concepts in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, I am still learning it and will keep you posted if I found something new.

Describing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Public Sector

Scope Item for PSM-Budget Management:

Following scope item was automatically activated when I selected the Public Sector Management.

PSM Bundle Activated

 

 

 

 

When we activate Public Sector Management in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud , it automatically setup and configure SAP Budget Management (known as PSM-Funds Management in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud) , SAP Grants Management and Fund Accounting.

Budget Management in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud:

In this knowledge article, I will shed some light on my experience with execution and setting up Budget Management in Public cloud. There are lot things which have been set to work differently in SAP Budget Management. If the public sector budget management solution is activated, transactions impacting the budget require budget-relevant account assignments. Often the budget-relevant account assignments are not manually entered, they are derived by the system.

Key Differences between SAP S/4HANA Private and Public Cloud from Budgeting Perspective:

Budget management reuses the master data from other SAP components in combination with PSM-specific master data as account assignments for budget-relevant transaction postings. I will illustrate this statement with below table. 

I also could not see any option to post payment and commitment budget separately in Budget Management, if anyone has come across such functionality in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud then please do let me know.

SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud

SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

In SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud or even in ECC, for Funds Management system to work, two FM account assignments were mandatory namely Commitment Items and Fund Center

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, Budget management works with a mandatory combination of Fund , Budget Period, Cost Center & Account Number

In SAP S/4HANA Private cloud, there could be one to one, one to many or many to one relationship between Commitment Item to General Ledger, you could automatically derive Commitment item if there was 1:1 relationship .

In SAP S/4HANA Public cloud is has flipped altogether. You can only have 1:1 relationship between your General ledger and Budget Account . The same concept is applicable for cost center.

In SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud, the department for which budget was posted was known as Fund Center.

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, Fund center has been replaced with Cost Center as it has reused the cost accounting assignment.

In SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud, the budget head for which budget was posted in combination with Fund center was known as Commitment item.

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, 

the G/L account is used as master data for the budget account.

Use of Fund was not mandatory.

Use of Fund is mandatory for posting the budget, the type of fund external or internal plays a critical role here.

Budget period was optional in PSM-FM and you had to activate it to be used.

Budget period is mandatory in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

WBS never existed in PSM-FM

We can optionally use WBS element in Budget management to post Budget.

Use of grant account assignment was only needed in PSM-FM Budgeting workbench when SAP GM-GTE was activated.

Use of Grant account assignment is mandatory in Budget Management. 

Funded Program was available in PSM-FM budgeting and derivation rule.

Funded Program does not exist in Budget management.

Important Master Data Object in Budget Management:

Following are the important master data objects in Budget management with their pictorial overview.

Fund Master Data:

Fund master data is an important master data object in budget management without which Budget can not be posted. It is maintained by app SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library

Budget Account Master Data:

FIORI App which is used to create budget account which is nothing but a General Ledger Account is SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library

Budget Period:

Budget Period master data is managed via FIORI App Manage Budget period. The budget period master data is pre-delivered in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud , Budget periods have been maintained for the entire FY e.g. FY 2026 is represented by one budget period, you may create your own master data for Budget period, if you want to maintain and post budget month wise.

 

I will not go into the details of other master data objects in PSM-Budget Management like Cost Center and WBS as they are self explanatory .

Derivation Rule in S/4HANA Public Cloud:

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, derivation rules are maintained very differently unlike Private Cloud where we had FMDERIVER/FMDERIVE to maintain derivation rules, we have a dedicated FIORI App to Maintain Substitution/Validation Rules for Public Sector as shown below.

 

Posting Budget in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud:

Budgeting is a holistic process and it follows many critical steps like preparing, planning, consumption monitoring, returns and supplements etc. It has been described in below flow diagram which has been taken from SAP help library.

In Budget management, budget is posted via following app. 

There are lot of functionalities within above app and it would require me to write a separate blog on the future and processes available within Budget posting app. For now, please just bear in that the original budget, supplement, transfers or return etc. happens using this app.

Budget Management Integration with SAP Analytical Cloud (SAC):

The SAP_COM_0761 communication scenario offers the integration of Public Sector Management (PSM) on SAP SAP S/4HANA Cloud as a data provider with SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC).

This scenario offers OData services for the following:

  • Fund and hierarchy
  • Grant and hierarchy
  • Budget period and hierarchy
  • Actuals and Commitment

The following figure provides an overview of how to do the following:

  • The master data, budget, and actuals and commitment are transferred between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • The budget is prepared in SAP Analytics Cloud

Recording Commitments/Actuals:

In public sector organisations, no business activity that will result in a future payment can be approved without an available budget. Therefore, it is crucial to reserve budget funds as early as possible to ensure proper financial planning. When governments anticipate future payments, typically due to an operational commitment document, they must formally set aside the required budget. This reservation process, known as "commitment updating," is documented via a financial budget consumption record and is subject to active availability checks.

In some instances, a formal commitment document may not exist, meaning the actual expenditure directly consumes the budget. Both scenarios – whether the spending is linked to a prior commitment or directly consumes the budget – are referred to as "actual updating." The combination of commitment and actual postings constitutes the total budget consumption.

Public Sector Management (PSM)-specific information for both commitment and actual transactions is recorded in the universal journal (table ACDOCA). This involves adding the relevant PSM account assignments, master data, and budgetary attributes to the universal journal entries when documents are posted. Specifically, commitment postings (such as purchase requisitions, purchase orders, or earmarked funds) update ledger 0E, while actual expenditure documents update ledger 0L.

You can learn a lot more about updating commitment/actuals using below link 
Recording Commitments and Actuals

Recording Commitments:

We will create a PO to record commitment of 5000 USD on budget dimensions for which I have posted budget. Commitment update logic that I have observed in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud PSM conforms to PBET (Period Based Encumbrance Tracking) . If you want to learn more about it then please read my blog on PBET
Period Based Encumbrance Tracking in PSM-FM - SAP Community

Recording Actuals:

Posting invoices, expense JVs , GRNs impacts the actuals and converts commitments into actual postings. I have posted a GRN of 60% of PO value which was 5000 USD. I have posted 3000 USD as GRN and now we will see effect of recording actuals on reporting and commitments which were already recorded.

Manage AVC Exceptions:

There is a FIORI app to make exceptions for cost center or budget account if you want to exclude them from budget check. You can do it for a specific user and control address for a given date range and assign a tolerance profile which does not check budget etc.

Budget Management Integration:

You can learn about budget management integration with other components within SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system using below link. The link offers deep dive into integration of budget management with Finance, SuccessFactors, Procurement, Concur, Plant maintenance etc.

Describing Finance Integration

Limitations in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Budget Management:

During my exploration of Budget Management functionality , I came across following limitations in Budget Management Module.

1. No segregation of payment and commitment budgets
2. Budget structure functionality has not been offered in Budget Management
3. I didn’t see multi-year budget functionality in Budget Management
4. AVC is not as highly configurable as it is in Private cloud
5. Budget commitment does not happen when you park or save as complete an Invoice or a JV

Finishing Lines:

In summary, while both SAP Budget Management in the Public Cloud and SAP Funds Management are powerful tools for financial control, they cater to distinct strategic imperatives. The former offers a modern, cloud-native approach aligned with future-proof digital transformation, while the latter provides established, comprehensive functionalities. Understanding these differences is paramount for organisations navigating their journey in the SAP ecosystem. Please let me know if there are improvements or corrections needed 😊.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition – All Communication Scenarios in One Place (Bookmark)
entry.content:
Make sure to bookmark this blog so you can easily revisit all communication scenarios in one place. Share it with your friends and colleagues—it’s a handy resource when searching across different SAP portals.

What is a Communication Scenario ?

A Communication Scenario in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud is a predefined, packaged set of:

  • Inbound/Outbound APIs (OData, SOAP)
  • Business events (for event-based integration)
  • Message types
  • Authorizations

These scenarios act as the integration between SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and other SAP Products or external systems.

Think of a communication scenario as a “bundle” :

These are the APIs and permissions this external system is allowed to use, and here’s how the communication should happen.

Each Communication Scenario is delivered by SAP S/4HANA Public Product Release and cannot be modified—but you can activate and use them.

Key Components of a Communication Scenario

A typical scenario includes:

1. Communication User

A technical user used for authentication.
Supports:

  • Basic Authentication
  • OAuth 2.0 (strongly recommended)

2. Communication System

Represents the external (non‑SAP) or SAP system you are integrating with.
Defines:

  • Hostname / Token URLs
  • Outbound target URLs
  • Authentication method

3. Communication Arrangement

This is where everything comes together.

You assign:

  • The Communication Scenario
  • The Communication System
  • The Communication User
  • Any specific parameters (e.g., service URLs, outbound destinations)

If you think of it simply:

Scenario = Template
Arrangement = Active Configuration

Why Are Communication Scenarios Important?

In S/4HANA Public Cloud, customers cannot create custom communication scenarios like in on‑premise or Private Cloud. This strict governance ensures:

  • Security and compliance
  • System stability
  • Standardized integration patterns
  • Ease of lifecycle management during upgrades

So instead of customizing scenarios, you configure and connect the right SAP‑delivered scenarios.

APIs usually come with a predefined communication scenario. To use the APIs, you first need to create a communication arrangement based on the communication scenario.

How to Configure a Communication Scenario: Step-by-Step

Step 1: Create a Communication User

SAP Fiori App: Maintain Communication Users

  • Create user → assign password → store securely
  • Or create an OAuth technical user (preferred)

Step 2: Create a Communication System

SAP Fiori App: Communication Systems

Here you:

  • Assign the communication user
  • Maintain authentication method
  • Provide target host details
  • Configure OAuth token endpoint (if applicable)

Step 3: Create Communication Arrangement

SAP Fiori App: Communication Arrangements

Steps:

  1. Select the communication scenario (e.g., SAP_COM_0008)
  2. Assign the communication system
  3. Review/activate inbound and outbound services
  4. Copy Service URLs for external system use

Once activated, the APIs are ready for use.

Testing Your Scenario

You can test APIs using tools like:

  • SAP Business Accelerator Hub (API Hub)
  • SAP Integration Suite (CPI)
  • SAP API Management
  • SAP BTP  (Destinations, Side by Side Extensions, Extensibility Development)
  • Custom integrations

Check which communication scenario fits to your purposes best.

The following list gives you an overview of the most important communication scenarios available today

SAP_COM_0227(Deprecated) Marketing - Campaign Execution - Twitter Integration

SAP_COM_0632Web Dynpro ABAP ALV for PDF Signing Integration (SAP_COM_0632)
SAP_COM_0914Workforce Availability Integration
